reviewer: new-demo build wave (headless Chrome real download+inference on index + all 4 ladder pages, cached-reload auto-init probe, mobile+desktop overflow check, light/dark screenshots)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference verified. Runnability was confirmed two ways before building: (1) Python onnxruntime on facenet.onnx — 128-D output, cos(identical)=1.0; on the 4 bundled synthetic faces same-identity(augmented)=0.86–0.99 vs different-identity=−0.11…0.22. (2) In-browser ORT-web WASM harness (onnxruntime-web@1.21.0, dynamic import) — 128-D, cos(person-a vs person-b)=0.002. transformers.js@3.7.5 has NO FaceNet/InceptionResnetV1 class, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a Web Worker (isolated per-worker pin, like yolov10-detection). Headless build verify: fresh profile → Download → ready → Compare produced sim 0.953 'Likely the same person' (person-a-1 vs person-a-2), 0 console errors, 0 net failures.
rubric.notes: Model astaileyyoung/facenet-onnx (MIT), NHWC [1,160,160,3] prewhitened input → [1,128] embedding, L2-normalized in the worker.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed on every page. Same-person pairs land high, different-person low, across all rungs: basics 0.953 (same person, two photos); practical 0.953 → MATCH signal; wild ranked the gallery and picked the true match Person A at cosine 0.953 for reference person-a-2; multi-model detected+cropped person A vs person C → 0.218 'Likely different people' (correct). 128-D embeddings render as signed bars in 'See inside'; cosine + Euclidean distance shown numerically.
rubric.notes: Cosine threshold 0.50 (same) / 0.32 (borderline) cleanly separates the verified same/different bands.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (astaileyyoung/facenet-onnx linked to its card), declares-task=pass (image-feature-extraction), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (fp32), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), input/output tensor shapes, and honestly notes transformers.js has no FaceNet class — no invented API surface.
rubric.notes: fp32 ONNX; the int8 path is not used (fp32 is the shipped, verified build).

rubric.evidence: conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass. Cached-reload probe (single profile, two navigations): first visit showed Download → downloaded → ready; SECOND visit auto-initialised to state='ready' with NO Download button present and Compare enabled, 0 console errors. The worker fetches facenet.onnx THROUGH Cache Storage (facenet-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/astaileyyoung/facenet-onnx/' and the shared loader auto-inits + the per-model clear-cache control works.
rubric.notes: Every page uses createModelLoader — no hand-rolled Load button.

rubric.evidence: conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams from the worker (content-length) into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Fresh-profile load shows the honest Download state — no silent large download.
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: the model is WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) so it runs everywhere with no GPU needed — verified running on the no-GPU headless runner.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, tabindex, Enter/Space handlers), dropzones + file inputs, Compare/Enrol/Verify/Find buttons, the practical threshold <input type=range> (updates the decision live on cached embeddings — no re-inference), and the wild gallery. buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass.
rubric.notes: Threshold slider re-decides from cached cosine — INP-safe.

rubric.evidence: All similarity numbers come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. The distinct, sensible cosines per page (0.953 same vs 0.218 different vs the ranked gallery) prove the output tracks the real embeddings, not a placeholder.

conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. Sample faces have descriptive alt; canvases carry aria-label; the similarity gauge and embedding strips have role=img + aria-label; status uses role=status aria-live=polite. Sample thumbs and dropzones are keyboard-operable (Enter/Space).
rubric.notes: Synthetic-faces context is announced in each sample-strip aria-label.

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured mobile (360×740 DPR3) horizontal overflow = 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages; desktop 1280×800 light+dark and mobile light screenshots reviewed — pair grid stacks cleanly, text legible, gauge/embedding strips fit, no clipped controls. Layout uses flex:1 1 240px + min-inline-size:0 (css-layout guidance) so the two face columns wrap instead of overflowing.
rubric.notes: aspect-ratio:1/1 face frames reserve space; no 100vh traps.

rubric.evidence: Inference + prewhiten preprocessing run in a Web Worker (transferred ImageBitmaps, zero-copy). The main thread only draws previews (drawImage) and computes a 128-element dot product for cosine — trivial. Interactive controls (threshold slider, gallery ranking) operate on cached embeddings with no re-inference, so no blocking loop on the main thread. FaceNet WASM embed latency is sub-second per face on the headless runner.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes (heavy work off the main thread).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel canvas grid no horizontal overflow on mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items containing wide content so they shrink instead of overflowing; prefer logical properties and aspect-ratio to reserve media space.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the two face columns use flex:1 1 240px with min-inline-size:0 and wrap on narrow viewports; face frames use aspect-ratio:1/1. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360×740 on all pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript off the main thread (into a worker) so interactions stay responsive; don't run heavy loops during interactions.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — all ONNX inference + prewhiten preprocessing run in a dedicated module worker (raw ORT-web); embeddings are transferred back and the main thread only does cheap cosine + drawImage. Threshold/ranking re-decide from cached embeddings (no re-inference).

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible labelled controls status live region keyboard operable sample thumbnails
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give every control an accessible name and keyboard operation; announce async state in a role=status aria-live region; label non-text visualizations.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — sample thumbs/dropzones are role=button + Enter/Space operable, buttons have text names, the loader status is role=status aria-live=polite, and canvases/gauge/embedding strips carry aria-labels.

openQuestions: FaceNet WASM is fp32 (~91 MB). Is a smaller/quantised face-embedding ONNX (e.g. MobileFaceNet) worth adding as a lighter variant demo?
A WebGPU (ORT-web EP) path could speed batch gallery embedding — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a MobileFaceNet / smaller face-embedding variant as a lighter (<20 MB) alternative demo if a browser-runnable ONNX exists.
Add a live-webcam capture path (lib/capture-ux.js) to the practical unlock demo, user-initiated, so a probe can come from the camera.

summary: New demo: on-device face SIMILARITY via FaceNet (astaileyyoung/facenet-onnx, MIT) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a worker. Index + 4 ladder pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model), all verified with real in-browser inference, cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity. Framed honestly as similarity, not identity or surveillance; synthetic faces only.
